methods.method_name: Culturing and Preparation of Surirella ovalis
methods.description: Process for isolating and culturing Surirella ovalis, and preparing samples for various microscopy techniques.

steps.description: Surirella ovalis (Breb) was isolated from a local stream, and unialgal cultures were grown under constant illumination at 18~ in diatom medium containing vitamins.
steps.searchable_summary: isolate and culture Surirella ovalis in diatom medium with vitamins

steps.description: For transmission microscopy, the cells were fixed in 1% glutaraldehyde made up in the medium; about 5 s later, a small vol of 2% osmium tetroxide was added, and the cells were fixed concurrently in glutaraldehyde and osmium tetroxide for 20 min.
steps.searchable_summary: fix cells for transmission microscopy using glutaraldehyde and osmium tetroxide

steps.description: The cells were washed in water, then dehydrated slowly in acetone and flat embedded in Spurr's resin.
steps.searchable_summary: wash, dehydrate, and embed cells in Spurr's resin

steps.description: Sections were cut on diamond knives and collected on Formvar-coated grids, then stained with uranyl acetate and lead citrate before examination in a Philips EM 200 electron microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: cut and stain sections for electron microscopy

steps.description: For high voltage electron microscopy, 0.3-0.5-tzm thick sections were collected on Formvar-coated slot grids. The sections were stained for 20 min in methanolic uranyl acetate and for 5 min in lead citrate, then examined at 1,000 kV.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare and stain sections for high voltage electron microscopy
outputs: stained sections for high voltage electron microscopy

steps.description: For scanning electron microscopy, cells were concentrated by centrifugation, treated with concentrated nitric acid for 5 min, washed several times, and dried on glass cover slips.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare cells for scanning electron microscopy using centrifugation and nitric acid
outputs: prepared cells for scanning electron microscopy

steps.description: The specimens were coated with carbon and gold, and viewed in a Cambridge Stereoscan $4 scanning electron microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: coat specimens with carbon and gold for scanning electron microscopy
outputs: coated specimens for scanning electron microscopy

steps.description: Light micrographs were taken with Zeiss Nomarski differential interference contrast optics.
steps.searchable_summary: capture light micrographs using Nomarski optics
